item.title: Jeffrey Soriano takes oath as new ACT-CIS rep after Edvic Yap's resignation
item.description: Jeffrey Soriano took his oath as ACT-CIS Party List Representative during House plenary session on Wednesday, replacing Edvic Yap, who resigned last month.

item.title: Families of EJK victims, human rights groups mark first year of Duterte’s arrest with protest
item.description: Families of victims of extrajudicial killings (EJK) and several human rights organizations gathered along Katipunan Avenue in Quezon City on Wednesday afternoon to commemorate the first anniversary of the arrest of former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte for alleged crimes against humanity.

item.title: CA panel strikes out Marcoleta’s use of word ‘satsat’ in meeting
item.description: The panel was considering the ad interim appointments of 70 generals and senior officers of the Armed Forces of the Philippines when Marcoleta brought up the topic of the West Philippine Sea, an issue he has engaged in heated debate on at the Senate in recent weeks.

item.title: Fisherfolk to get P3,000 fuel subsidy as oil supply woes continue: BFAR
item.description: MANILA — Small scale fisherfolk will receive a P3,000 fuel subsidy from the government as the sector reels from a steep hike in fuel prices, the Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources (BFAR) said on Wednesday.

item.title: VP Duterte’s lawyers enter appearance before House justice panel
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte's legal team for impeachment proceedings against her at the House include lawyers from the Fortun, Narvasa and Salazar firm as well as two lawyers who have served as her spokespersons.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
